scenes.summary: The wind smelled of salt and fear
beats: Beat 1: The wind smelled of salt and fear
Beat 2: Families stood at the edge of the Red Sea, their bundles at their feet, children clutching their parents’ hands
Beat 3: Behind them, in the distance, rose a cloud of dust from Pharaoh’s chariots racing closer and closer
Beat 4: A little boy named Eli tugged on his mother’s robe
Beat 5: “Mama… are they coming for us?” His mother knelt and held his shoulders
Beat 6: “Stay close to me, Eli
Beat 7: God brought us this far
Beat 8: ” Nearby, Miriam, Moses’ sister, stood on a rock and called out, “Do not panic! Stay together!” But panic spread anyway
Beat 9: “There’s nowhere to run!” shouted one man
Beat 10: “The sea is in front of us and soldiers behind us!” cried another
Beat 11: “Wasn’t Egypt terrible enough?” an old woman said
Beat 12: “Now we will die in the wilderness!” Moses turned and looked at the people
Beat 13: He saw fear in their faces, and he felt it too, like a stone in his chest
Beat 14: Then he lifted his eyes to the darkening sky and prayed
Beat 15: “Lord,” he whispered, “Your people are afraid
Beat 16: Show us Your way
Beat 17: ” In his heart, he heard God’s command: Tell the people to move forward
Beat 18: Lift your staff over the sea
Beat 19: I will make a path
Beat 20: Moses stepped up onto a rise of sand where everyone could see him
Beat 21: He raised his hand
Beat 1: “Listen to me!” he called
Beat 2: The crowd quieted, though children still whimpered and horses still thundered in the distance
Beat 3: “Do not be afraid,” Moses said, his voice strong now
Beat 4: “Stand firm, and you will see how the Lord saves you today! The Egyptians you see now—you will not see them again
Beat 5: ” A young girl near the front blinked
Beat 6: “How can we go forward? There’s water!” Moses pointed toward the sea
Beat 7: “The Lord will fight for us
Beat 8: We must trust Him
Beat 9: ” He lifted his staff high over the Red Sea
Beat 10: At first, nothing happened
Beat 11: Then a wind began to blow
Beat 12: Not a gentle breeze—this was a mighty wind that roared like a lion
Beat 13: It whipped cloaks and scarves, bent palm trees, and drove spray from the waves
Beat 14: All night it blew
Beat 15: The people wrapped their arms around themselves and watched in awe as the sea began to change
Beat 16: The water pulled back
Beat 17: It split
Beat 18: A pathway of wet earth appeared where water had been moments before
Beat 19: On both sides, walls of water rose and shimmered in the moonlight, higher than any palace in Egypt
Beat 20: Eli gasped, eyes wide
Beat 21: “Mama… the sea is standing up!” His mother’s voice shook
Beat 1: “A miracle
Beat 2: ” Miriam laughed in wonder
Beat 3: “He made a road in the sea!” Moses lowered his staff and shouted, “Go! Walk through! Keep moving!” The people stepped forward carefully at first, then with growing courage
Beat 4: Sand squished under sandals
Beat 5: Carts rattled
Beat 6: Babies cried
Beat 7: Sheep bleated
Beat 8: Grandparents leaned on staffs while older children helped carry bundles
Beat 9: As they entered the path, many looked up at the walls of water and hurried faster
Beat 10: A man whispered to his wife, “I can see fish in there!” A little girl reached out a hand before her father pulled it back
Beat 11: “No touching the water,” he said, trying to sound brave
Beat 12: “Just keep walking
Beat 13: ” All night they crossed
Beat 14: At the rear, Moses and a few strong men guided the slowest travelers
Beat 15: Above them, a pillar of cloud moved behind the Israelites, standing between them and the Egyptian army like a giant shield
Beat 16: On the far side of the sea, the first families climbed onto dry land
Beat 17: They turned and watched as more and more people emerged, tired but alive
Beat 18: Eli stumbled onto the shore and flopped into the sand, laughing with relief
Beat 19: “We made it!” His mother kissed his forehead
Beat 20: “Yes, we made it
Beat 21: ” But then came a shout from the back

scenes.summary: “The Egyptians! They’re entering the path!” Pharaoh had not given up
beats: Beat 1: “The Egyptians! They’re entering the path!” Pharaoh had not given up
Beat 2: His chariots charged into the seabed road, wheels pounding the earth
Beat 3: Soldiers yelled and cracked whips, trying to catch the escaping people
Beat 4: Pharaoh stood in his chariot and pointed his sword
Beat 5: “Forward! They are trapped now!” One of his captains hesitated
Beat 6: “My lord… this is no ordinary road
Beat 7: Their God—” “Forward!” Pharaoh roared
Beat 8: The chariots raced in
Beat 9: But soon the ground turned sticky and uneven
Beat 10: Wheels sank and twisted
Beat 11: Horses screamed and reared
Beat 12: A soldier cried out, “The wheels are breaking!” Another shouted, “Turn back! Their God is fighting us!” On the far shore, the Israelites watched, breathless
Beat 13: Some clung to each other
Beat 14: Some prayed aloud
Beat 15: Moses stood at the water’s edge, staff in hand, waiting
Beat 16: Then God spoke to him again: Stretch out your hand over the sea, so the waters may return
Beat 17: Moses raised his staff one more time
Beat 18: The wind stopped
Beat 19: For one heartbeat, all was silent
Beat 20: Then the walls of water crashed down with a thunder like mountains breaking
Beat 21: Waves roared over the chariots, over horses, over armor and spears

scenes.summary: The sea closed and rolled, deep and wide as before
beats: Beat 1: The sea closed and rolled, deep and wide as before
Beat 2: When the sun rose, the shore glowed gold
Beat 3: The enemy was gone
Beat 4: The people were safe
Beat 5: For a moment, no one moved
Beat 6: Then a single voice began to sing—Miriam’s
Beat 7: “Sing to the Lord, for He is highly exalted!” Women gathered around her with tambourines, and soon the whole camp joined in
Beat 8: Their voices rose over the water, joyful and free
Beat 9: “The horse and rider He has thrown into the sea!” Children danced
Beat 10: Old men laughed
Beat 11: Mothers cried happy tears
Beat 12: Fathers lifted toddlers into the air
Beat 13: Eli looked up at Moses, who stood quietly nearby, eyes shining with gratitude
Beat 14: “Did you know God would do that?” Eli asked
Beat 15: Moses knelt so he was eye level with the boy
Beat 16: “I knew God would make a way,” he said softly
Beat 17: “I just didn’t know what it would look like
Beat 18: ” Eli thought about that
Beat 19: “So… when we’re scared, we trust Him first?” Moses smiled
Beat 20: “Yes
Beat 21: We listen, we obey, and we move forward

scenes.summary: ” Miriam came over, still holding her tambourine
beats: Beat 1: ” Miriam came over, still holding her tambourine
Beat 2: “Come dance, Eli! Today is a day to remember forever
Beat 3: ” Eli grinned and ran to the circle of singers
Beat 4: As the people celebrated, Moses looked across the sea one last time
Beat 5: Behind them was slavery, fear, and chains
Beat 6: Ahead was a long journey through the wilderness—but also hope, promise, and a future God would guide them to step by step
Beat 7: He turned toward the path ahead and called out, “People of Israel, gather your families
Beat 8: We go forward together
Beat 9: ” The people answered with one voice: “Together!” And under the bright morning sky, they began walking into freedom

Beat 10: › Ensure that at leat 40% of this story helps set context as to who moses was and why he was running away • In Egypt, long before the sea split in two, there was a baby boy hidden in a basket
Beat 11: His name was Moses
Beat 12: At that time, Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, was afraid of the growing number of Israelites
Beat 13: So he gave a cruel order: Hebrew baby boys were to be killed
Beat 14: Moses’ mother, Jochebed, could not bear to lose him
Beat 15: She placed him in a basket coated with pitch and set it among the reeds of the Nile River
Beat 16: Moses’ big sister, Miriam, watched from a distance
Beat 17: Soon, Pharaoh’s daughter came down to the river with her servants
Beat 18: “What is that in the reeds?” she asked
Beat 19: A servant pulled out the basket
Beat 20: Inside was a crying baby
Beat 21: Pharaoh’s daughter’s face softened

scenes.summary: “This is one of the Hebrew children
beats: Beat 1: “This is one of the Hebrew children
Beat 2: ” Miriam quickly stepped forward
Beat 3: “Should I find a Hebrew woman to nurse the baby for you?” Pharaoh’s daughter nodded
Beat 4: “Yes
Beat 5: Bring her
Beat 6: ” So Miriam brought their own mother
Beat 7: Jochebed cared for Moses until he was older, then Moses went to live in Pharaoh’s palace
Beat 8: He grew up wearing fine clothes and learning the ways of Egypt
Beat 9: But deep inside, he knew he was Hebrew
Beat 10: Years passed
Beat 11: One day, Moses went out and saw an Egyptian slave master beating a Hebrew worker
Beat 12: “Stop!” Moses shouted
Beat 13: He helped the girls water their sheep
Beat 14: They returned home early, and their father, Jethro, asked, “Why are you back so soon?” “An Egyptian helped us,” they said
Beat 15: Jethro invited Moses to stay
Beat 16: Moses married Jethro’s daughter, Zipporah, and became a shepherd
Beat 17: For many years, he lived quietly in Midian
Beat 18: He had a family
Beat 19: He watched sheep
Beat 20: He likely hoped his old life was over
Beat 21: But God had not forgotten Moses

scenes.summary: One day, while Moses was near Mount Horeb, he saw something strange: a bush on fire that did not burn up
beats: Beat 1: One day, while Moses was near Mount Horeb, he saw something strange: a bush on fire that did not burn up
Beat 2: Moses stepped closer
Beat 3: “I must see this amazing thing
Beat 4: ” Then a voice called from the bush, “Moses! Moses!” He trembled
Beat 5: “Here I am
Beat 6: ” “Do not come any closer,” God said
Beat 7: “Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground
Beat 8: I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob
Beat 9: ” Moses covered his face in fear
Beat 10: God said, “I have seen the suffering of My people in Egypt
Beat 11: I have heard their cries
Beat 12: I am sending you to Pharaoh to bring My people out of Egypt
Beat 13: ” Moses stared at the burning bush
Beat 14: “Me? But who am I to go to Pharaoh?” “I will be with you,” God answered
Beat 15: “What if they ask Your name?” Moses said
Beat 16: God replied, “I AM WHO I AM
Beat 17: Tell them I AM has sent you
Beat 18: ” Moses still hesitated
Beat 19: “Please, Lord… I am not a good speaker
Beat 20: ” God said, “Your brother Aaron will help you speak
Beat 21: Go

scenes.summary: I will help both of you
beats: Beat 1: I will help both of you
Beat 2: ” So Moses returned to Egypt, the place he had once run from
Beat 3: He was no longer running to save only himself
Beat 4: Now he was leading a rescue
Beat 5: When Moses and Aaron stood before Pharaoh, Moses said, “The Lord, the God of Israel, says: Let My people go
Beat 6: ” Pharaoh laughed
Beat 7: “Who is this Lord? I do not know Him, and I will not let Israel go
Beat 8: ” Then came ten terrible plagues: water turning to blood, frogs, gnats, flies, sickness, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and finally the death of the firstborn
Beat 9: Pharaoh’s hard heart broke only after the last plague, and he finally shouted, “Go! Leave my land!” The Israelites left in a hurry, carrying unleavened dough and whatever they could gather
Beat 10: After hundreds of years of slavery, they were free
Beat 11: But soon Pharaoh changed his mind
Beat 12: “What have we done?” he barked at his officers
Beat 13: “We have let our slaves go!” He ordered his chariots prepared and raced after them
Beat 14: That is how Moses, once a baby in a basket, once a prince, once a frightened runaway, came to stand before the Red Sea with a nation behind him and an army closing in
Beat 15: The wind smelled of salt and fear
Beat 16: Families crowded the shore
Beat 17: Children cried
Beat 18: Carts creaked
Beat 19: Sheep bleated
Beat 20: A little boy named Eli clutched his mother’s hand
Beat 21: “Mama, are the soldiers coming?” “Yes,” she whispered
Beat 1: “Stay close
Beat 2: ” People began shouting at Moses
Beat 3: “Were there no graves in Egypt?” one man cried
Beat 4: “Did you bring us here to die?” “We told you this would happen!” shouted another
Beat 5: Moses felt the weight of every frightened voice
Beat 6: He lifted his eyes and prayed
Beat 7: God answered him: Tell the people to move forward
Beat 8: Lift your staff over the sea
Beat 9: Moses turned to the crowd and raised his hand
Beat 10: “Do not be afraid! Stand firm, and you will see the Lord save you today!” A girl near the front yelled, “Save us how? There’s water in front of us!” Moses pointed his staff toward the sea
Beat 11: “The Lord will fight for us
Beat 12: Keep moving!” He stretched out his hand
Beat 13: A mighty wind began to blow, stronger and stronger, all night long
Beat 14: The sea churned, then pulled back
Beat 15: Water rose on both sides like shining walls, and a path of dry ground appeared between them
Beat 16: Eli gasped
Beat 17: “Mama! The sea is standing up!” Miriam laughed with joy
Beat 18: “Make way! Walk through!” The people stepped into the path
Beat 19: At first slowly, then faster
Beat 20: Some stared wide-eyed at fish flickering in the watery walls
Beat 21: Others prayed as they walked

scenes.summary: “Hold my hand,” said a father to his daughter
beats: Beat 1: “Hold my hand,” said a father to his daughter
Beat 2: “I’m scared,” she whispered
Beat 3: “So am I,” he said
Beat 4: “But keep going
Beat 5: ” At the back, Pharaoh’s army reached the sea path and charged in
Beat 6: “After them!” Pharaoh shouted
Beat 7: Chariot wheels thundered over the seabed
Beat 8: But soon the ground turned muddy and heavy
Beat 9: Wheels jammed and snapped
Beat 10: An Egyptian soldier cried, “My lord, something is wrong!” Another shouted, “Their God is fighting us! Turn back!” On the far shore, the last Israelites scrambled to safety
Beat 11: Moses stood at the water’s edge, waiting for God’s command
Beat 12: Then it came: Stretch out your hand again
Beat 13: Moses lifted his staff
Beat 14: The wind stopped
Beat 15: For a heartbeat, all was still
Beat 16: Then the walls of water crashed down with a roar
Beat 17: Waves swallowed chariots, horses, and soldiers
Beat 18: When the sea settled, the army was gone
Beat 19: Morning light spread over the shore
Beat 20: The Israelites were safe
Beat 21: For a moment, no one spoke

scenes.summary: Then Miriam took a tambourine and sang, “Sing to the Lord, for He is highly exalted!” Women joined her with dancing and music
beats: Beat 1: Then Miriam took a tambourine and sang, “Sing to the Lord, for He is highly exalted!” Women joined her with dancing and music
Beat 2: Children laughed and jumped
Beat 3: Old men wept with relief
Beat 4: Families hugged each other tightly
Beat 5: Eli ran to Moses
Beat 6: “Did you know this would happen?” Moses knelt beside him
Beat 7: “I knew God would make a way,” he said
Beat 8: “I just didn’t know what it would look like
Beat 9: ” Eli thought hard about that
Beat 10: “So when we’re scared, we trust God and keep going?” Moses smiled
Beat 11: “Yes
Beat 12: We listen, we obey, and we move forward
Beat 13: ” Miriam called, “Eli! Come sing with us!” Eli grinned and ran to join the dancers
Beat 14: Moses looked back across the sea one last time
Beat 15: Behind them were chains and fear
Beat 16: Ahead of them was the wilderness, and it would not be easy
Beat 17: But they were no longer slaves
Beat 18: They were God’s people, traveling toward promise

Beat 19: He turned to the crowd and called, “Gather your families! We go forward together!” And the people answered, “Together!” So they walked on, led by the God who rescued a baby from a river, a runaway from the desert, and a nation through the sea

characters.name: Moses
characters.description: Hebrew leader chosen by God to free his people from Egyptian slavery.

characters.name: Miriam
characters.description: Moses’ sister, encourager and worship leader among the Israelites.

characters.name: Eli
characters.description: A little boy among the Israelites, symbolizing innocent hope and faith.

characters.name: Eli’s mother
characters.description: Protective mother who comforts and guides Eli during the escape.

characters.name: Pharaoh
characters.description: Egyptian king who opposes Israelite freedom and pursues them with his army.

characters.name: Pharaoh’s daughter
characters.description: Egyptian princess who rescues baby Moses from the Nile.

characters.name: Jochebed
characters.description: Moses’ mother who hides him and later nurses him for Pharaoh’s daughter.

characters.name: Aaron
characters.description: Moses’ brother who helps him speak to Pharaoh.

characters.name: Zipporah
characters.description: Moses’ wife, a Midianite shepherd’s daughter.

characters.name: Jethro
characters.description: Zipporah’s father, Moses’ father-in-law and Midianite leader.

characters.name: A young girl near the front
characters.description: Child among Israelites questioning how they can move forward through water.

characters.name: A man shouting
characters.description: Israelite man expressing fear and doubt at the Red Sea.

characters.name: An old woman
characters.description: Elderly Israelite expressing despair about dying in the wilderness.

characters.name: Pharaoh’s captain
characters.description: Officer in Pharaoh’s army hesitant about crossing the sea path.

characters.name: An Egyptian soldier
characters.description: Soldier reporting problems with chariot wheels in the seabed.

characters.name: A father with daughter
characters.description: Man comforting his scared daughter while crossing the Red Sea path.

subpanels.description: Wind blows fiercely by the Red Sea shore with looming dust cloud from chariots.
subpanels.prompt: Dynamic medium shot of families with bundles at shore edge, dust cloud chariots behind, wind tousling clothes and hair, dusk lighting with warm highlights. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): The wind smelled of salt and fear as danger approached.. Do not render speech bubbles in this subpanel.
subpanels.background_context_prompt: The wind smelled of salt and fear as danger approached.

subpanels.description: Eli tugs at his mother’s robe, looking scared; she kneels to comfort him.
subpanels.prompt: Close-up of small boy tugging mother’s robe with wide fearful eyes, mother kneeling holding his shoulders, warm ink shadows highlight their expressions. Dialogue context: Eli: Mama… are they coming for us? | Eli’s mother: Stay close to me, Eli.. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): “Mama… are they coming for us?” Eli asks worriedly..

bubbles.speaker: Eli
bubbles.text: Mama… are they coming for us?

bubbles.speaker: Eli’s mother
bubbles.text: Stay close to me, Eli.

subpanels.background_context_prompt: “Mama… are they coming for us?” Eli asks worriedly.

subpanels.description: Miriam stands on a rock warning the people; panicked crowd around her.
subpanels.prompt: Medium shot of Miriam on rock with tambourine raised, shouting comfort; behind her a diverse crowd expressing fear and panic, warm golden highlights. Dialogue context: Miriam: Do not panic! Stay together! | Man shouting: There’s nowhere to run!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Miriam calls out, but panic spreads despite her words..

bubbles.speaker: Miriam
bubbles.text: Do not panic! Stay together!

bubbles.speaker: Man shouting
bubbles.text: There’s nowhere to run!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Miriam calls out, but panic spreads despite her words.

subpanels.description: Moses looks over fearful crowd then prays, lifting eyes to darkening sky.
subpanels.prompt: Close-up on Moses’ serious face looking determined yet heavy-hearted; next frame him lifting arms skyward under darkening clouds, glowing warm highlight behind him. Dialogue context: Moses: Lord, Your people are afraid. Show us Your way.. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Moses prays for guidance amid the people’s fear..

bubbles.speaker: Moses
bubbles.text: Lord, Your people are afraid. Show us Your way.

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Moses prays for guidance amid the people’s fear.

subpanels.description: Moses raises his hand calling for silence; crowd quiets but distant sounds remain.
subpanels.prompt: Medium shot of Moses on a sand rise with arm raised commanding attention, crowd settling with children still whimpering and distant horse sounds, dusk warm contrast lighting. Dialogue context: Moses: Listen to me!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Moses demands attention to inspire hope..

bubbles.speaker: Moses
bubbles.text: Listen to me!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Moses demands attention to inspire hope.

subpanels.description: Moses speaks reassuringly to crowd, pointing toward the sea.
subpanels.prompt: Medium close-up on Moses firm and strong, hand gesturing to sea behind him, crowd listening uneasily, warm ink style with serious contrast. Dialogue context: Moses: Stand firm, the Lord saves today! You won’t see Egyptians again. | Young girl: How can we go forward? There’s water!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Moses tells them not to fear and trust God’s plan..

bubbles.speaker: Moses
bubbles.text: Stand firm, the Lord saves today! You won’t see Egyptians again.

bubbles.speaker: Young girl
bubbles.text: How can we go forward? There’s water!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Moses tells them not to fear and trust God’s plan.

subpanels.description: Moses lifts staff over the Red Sea; roaring wind whips hair and cloaks.
subpanels.prompt: Dynamic medium shot of Moses holding staff high; wind bending palm trees and swirling sea spray, silhouettes of people watching amazed, dramatic warm highlights on waves and fabric. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): A mighty wind begins to roar as Moses raises his staff.. Do not render speech bubbles in this subpanel.
subpanels.background_context_prompt: A mighty wind begins to roar as Moses raises his staff.

subpanels.description: Sea begins to pull back revealing a path flanked by towering water walls under moonlight.
subpanels.prompt: Wide shot from low angle of shimmering towering walls of water on each side forming a clear path, moonlight reflecting with warm golden accents, crowd watching wide-eyed including Eli. Dialogue context: Eli: Mama… the sea is standing up!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): The sea splits, revealing a miraculous pathway forward..

bubbles.speaker: Eli
bubbles.text: Mama… the sea is standing up!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: The sea splits, revealing a miraculous pathway forward.

subpanels.description: Miriam laughs joyfully calling it a miracle.
subpanels.prompt: Close-up on Miriam joyful and laughing, tambourine in hand; warm golden highlights contrast her bright expression against dark waving water walls behind. Dialogue context: Miriam: A miracle!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Miriam delights in the miracle before them..

bubbles.speaker: Miriam
bubbles.text: A miracle!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Miriam delights in the miracle before them.

subpanels.description: Moses lowers staff shouting for the people to move forward.
subpanels.prompt: Medium shot of Moses commanding, staff lowering, crowd stepping cautiously onto sand path flanked by high water walls, warm highlights on faces showing growing courage. Dialogue context: Moses: Go! Walk through! Keep moving!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Moses urges the people forward through the sea path..

bubbles.speaker: Moses
bubbles.text: Go! Walk through! Keep moving!

subpanels.description: People traverse sea path: carts rattle, children cry, animals bleat, elders lean on staffs.
subpanels.prompt: Wide angle medium shot showing diverse crowd moving forward on wet sand; babies in arms, sheep following, elders steady on staffs, warm tones on flowing robes and muddy feet. Dialogue context: Man whispering: I can see fish in there! | Father: No touching the water. Just keep walking.. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Everyone crosses, some racing in awe, others steady and cautious..

bubbles.speaker: Man whispering
bubbles.text: I can see fish in there!

bubbles.speaker: Father
bubbles.text: No touching the water. Just keep walking.

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Everyone crosses, some racing in awe, others steady and cautious.

subpanels.description: Moses and strong men guide slow travelers; pillar of cloud shields behind travelers.
subpanels.prompt: Medium shot of Moses with staff walking behind tired travelers, strong men helping; behind a massive white cloud pillar glowing warmly, separating Israelites and approaching army. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): A cloud pillar shields the people from the pursuing army.. Do not render speech bubbles in this subpanel.
subpanels.background_context_prompt: A cloud pillar shields the people from the pursuing army.

subpanels.description: Pharaoh shouts orders from chariot as soldiers charge into sea path.
subpanels.prompt: Dynamic medium shot of Pharaoh standing in chariot wielding sword, shouting fiercely, soldiers behind charging with whips cracking, glowing intense orange highlights from sunset. Dialogue context: Pharaoh: Forward! They are trapped now!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Pharaoh commands relentless pursuit through sea path..

bubbles.speaker: Pharaoh
bubbles.text: Forward! They are trapped now!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Pharaoh commands relentless pursuit through sea path.

subpanels.description: A hesitant captain warns about the strange road; Pharaoh rebukes angrily.
subpanels.prompt: Medium close-up on captain with worried expression confronting Pharaoh who roars back authoritatively; tense faces, high contrast lighting intensifying emotions. Dialogue context: Pharaoh’s captain: My lord… this is no ordinary road… | Pharaoh: Forward!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): The captain suspects divine intervention; Pharaoh ignores the warning..

bubbles.speaker: Pharaoh’s captain
bubbles.text: My lord… this is no ordinary road…

bubbles.speaker: Pharaoh
bubbles.text: Forward!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: The captain suspects divine intervention; Pharaoh ignores the warning.

subpanels.description: Chariot wheels sink into shifting seabed; horses scream and rear up.
subpanels.prompt: Wide shot of horses in chariots struggling as wheels stuck in sticky mud, armored soldiers struggling, tense lighting with warm highlights casting dramatic shadows. Dialogue context: Soldier: The wheels are breaking! Turn back!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): The ground betrays the pursuers; chariots get stuck..

bubbles.speaker: Soldier
bubbles.text: The wheels are breaking! Turn back!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: The ground betrays the pursuers; chariots get stuck.

subpanels.description: Moses stands calm at water’s edge, staff raised, waiting for God’s command.
subpanels.prompt: Close-up on Moses firm and focused, staff held high, silhouette against darkening sea and rising waves, warm rim light highlighting resolute expression. Dialogue context: God (voice): Stretch out your hand over the sea, so the waters may return.. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Moses prepares for God’s next instruction at critical moment..

bubbles.speaker: God (voice)
bubbles.text: Stretch out your hand over the sea, so the waters may return.

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Moses prepares for God’s next instruction at critical moment.

subpanels.description: Massive walls of water crash down swallowing Pharaoh’s chariots and army.
subpanels.prompt: Epic wide shot from low angle of towering crashing waves engulfing soldiers, horses, and chariots in dark sea with dynamic splashes and warm highlights at crests. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): The sea triumphantly closes, sweeping away the enemy.. Do not render speech bubbles in this subpanel.
subpanels.background_context_prompt: The sea triumphantly closes, sweeping away the enemy.

subpanels.description: The sea returns to quiet, rolling deep and wide as before at dawn.
subpanels.prompt: Tranquil long shot of calm sea at sunrise glowing gold along shoreline, no signs of army or chaos, warm golden palette contrasting deep blue ink sea. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): At sunrise, peace returns; the enemy is gone, the people safe.. Do not render speech bubbles in this subpanel.
subpanels.background_context_prompt: At sunrise, peace returns; the enemy is gone, the people safe.

subpanels.description: Miriam leads joyful singing with tambourine; women, children, and others join with praise.
subpanels.prompt: Medium group shot of Miriam smiling, tambourine raised, surrounded by singing women and dancing children, warm highlights glowing on joyful faces and flowing robes. Dialogue context: Miriam: Sing to the Lord, for He is highly exalted!. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Joyful voices rise in praise for the Lord’s great salvation..

bubbles.speaker: Miriam
bubbles.text: Sing to the Lord, for He is highly exalted!

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Joyful voices rise in praise for the Lord’s great salvation.

subpanels.description: Eli laughs with relief on shore; Moses kneels to comfort him, exchanging hopeful words.
subpanels.prompt: Close-up scene of Eli lying, laughing in sand, Moses kneeling beside him with calm, grateful eyes, warm lighting highlighting their expressions of hope and trust. Dialogue context: Eli: Did you know God would do that? | Moses: I knew God would make a way.. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): Hope blooms as Moses teaches trust amidst uncertainty..

bubbles.speaker: Eli
bubbles.text: Did you know God would do that?

bubbles.speaker: Moses
bubbles.text: I knew God would make a way.

subpanels.background_context_prompt: Hope blooms as Moses teaches trust amidst uncertainty.

Moses! | Moses: Here I am.. Background context caption (bottom horizontal text, not a speech bubble): God calls Moses by name from holy ground..
